step_1_context.text: If I had studied harder, I would have passed the exam. Unfortunately, I failed.

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: Use third conditional for unreal situations in the past — things that did not happen.
step_2_explanation.formula: If + past perfect, would have + past participle
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: Do not confuse with second conditional. Third conditional is always about the past.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: If I ___ earlier, I would have caught the bus.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Third conditional requires past perfect (had + past participle) in the if-clause.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: She ___ the job if she had applied on time.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: The result clause uses would have + past participle.

step_3_interactive_exercises.incorrect_sentence: If they would have called, I would have answered.
step_3_interactive_exercises.corrected_sentence: If they had called, I would have answered.
step_3_interactive_exercises.error_analysis: Never use 'would have' in the if-clause. Use past perfect: had called.
